page: Microsoft Azure E2as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EASv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 33 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with no complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance is strong, scoring 2703.88 in stress-ng tests, which places it in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is weak, scoring the same 2703.88 in stress-ng and 1959.0 in Geekbench, reflecting the physical single-core limitation. Memory bandwidth is relatively low, with read-only large block bandwidth at 32.67 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 22.32 GB/sec. Text processing and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels, with gzip compression reaching 26.04 GB/sec. Conversely, database operations and Redis SET operations show lower performance, with Redis achieving 2.03 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E2as_v4 offers a high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The primary tradeoff is the limited multi-core scaling due to the single physical core configuration. This server is best suited for light database caching, memory-intensive single-threaded applications, small-scale web serving, and basic text processing tasks where RAM capacity is prioritized over multi-threaded compute power.
description: The Microsoft Azure E2as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical core, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 33 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. While single-core performance is strong, scoring 2703.88 in stress-ng tests, multi-core performance is limited by the single physical core architecture. Memory bandwidth is modest, with large-block read speeds at 32.67 GB/sec. Qualitatively, this instance provides high memory density per vCPU, offering a cost-effective entry point for memory-bound applications. It is best suited for workloads such as light database caching, single-threaded memory-intensive tasks, text processing, and basic web serving where high RAM capacity is required but parallel compute demands are low.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, offering strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2as_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, ideal for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC compute and high memory density for memory-bound, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and 33 GB of local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core scaling
Features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core
Best suited for light database caching and memory-intensive applications
